title: Neon Genesis Evangelion Shonen or Seinen
display_title: Neon Genesis Evangelion Shonen or Seinen
wikitext:
[]: Neon Genesis Evangelion: Shonen or Seinen?             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Feb 06, 2010 1:40 pm I would like to read other peoples' opinion regarding the targeted demographics of Evangelion. I think it's shōnen. What do other think, and what are their reasons?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sat Feb 06, 2010 2:10 pm It's almost definitely shoujo. Except nah, your opinion is right.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Feb 06, 2010 2:26 pm It depends what episode you're watching.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Feb 06, 2010 2:31 pm I'd say it kicks off as shonen, but then ends up in vaguely-seinen territory by the end. Doesn't seem to be enough body horror, surgical tables, demons, entrails, or cyborgs to be pure seinen, tho'.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sat Feb 06, 2010 7:58 pm It's a seinen that's a satire of shonen and yaoi (Shinji is a uke).

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sat Feb 06, 2010 8:04 pm I'm pretty sure it's josei. Anno said he likes to read women's romance novels and that really comes through in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sat Feb 06, 2010 9:37 pm ZapX wrote: I'm pretty sure it's josei. Anno said he likes to read women's romance novels and that really comes through in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Feb 07, 2010 1:35 am Uriel Septim VII wrote: It's a seinen that's a satire of shonen and yaoi (Shinji is a uke).

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Feb 07, 2010 3:41 am ZapX wrote: I'm pretty sure it's josei. Anno said he likes to read women's romance novels and that really comes through in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Feb 07, 2010 11:53 am I think Evangelions genre is Shoseinjonen, yes that is the true genre of evangelion. I think we need more anime in that genre.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Feb 07, 2010 3:51 pm ZapX wrote: I'm pretty sure it's josei. Anno said he likes to read women's romance novels and that really comes through in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Feb 07, 2010 8:42 pm Defectron wrote: I think Evangelions genre is Shoseinjonen, yes that is the true genre of evangelion. I think we need more anime in that genre.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Feb 08, 2010 9:01 am I think it really depends on the episodes, to start: shonen up until around the descent arc, then develops into a seinen territory with episodes like splitting of the breast and Ambivalence, [with EoE being completely seinen] and some josei thrown in near the end. It's josei rather than shoujo to me due to what happens to Kaworu at the end of 24. I think the josei element comes through a lot more in certain scenes of EoE as well and of course the 'Do you want to become one with me' scene in Weaving a Story.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Feb 08, 2010 11:13 am In my opinion, Evangelion is a genre bender. Like how Gladiator was an R rated movie (suggested 17+) with 13-year-old male audiences in mind (as director Ridely Scott explains in the commentaries for the film). The film was meant to reach both adolescent and mature audiences. Whether or not it was his intention in the beginning, Hideaki Anno has managed to do the same thing with Neon Genesis Evangelion . Starting off as a shonen and finishing up as a seinen almost makes it seem as if Anno was trying to mirror adolescence growing up, as the character Shinji is forced to do in the series' progression.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Fri Feb 12, 2010 12:30 am I think it started off as a Shonen then became a Seinen later on as the series went on. Most anime and mangas where like that like Bastard!! for example.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Feb 12, 2010 8:00 am shonen (1-15) -> seinen (16-23) -> yaoi (24)-> Existentialism (25-26)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Feb 12, 2010 9:49 am ran1 wrote: Existentialism (25-26)

[]:             Quote Post by JupiterCallisto » Fri Feb 12, 2010 3:44 pm I think the anime has no definite audience. It can be enjoyed by Shonen, Seinen or whoever the heck else wants to enjoy it. The manga is Shonen though. (Which can be kinda proven by the fact that Sadaniti skipped the um...hospital scene in the beginning of EoE because the manga is read by a younger audience.) Well that and the fact that it's carried in Shonen Ace (And didn't get any edgier when it moved to Young Ace.)

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Feb 12, 2010 6:58 pm Xard wrote: Existentialism is not anime genre!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 12, 2010 10:00 pm Zankoku na tenshi no you ni Shounen yo shinwa ni nare! It's a shounen the same way it's a giant robot show.
summary:
[]: Neon Genesis Evangelion: Shonen or Seinen?             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Feb 06, 2010 1:40 pm I would like to read other peoples' opinion regarding the targeted demographics of Evangelion. I think it's shōnen. What do other think, and what are their reasons?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sat Feb 06, 2010 2:10 pm It's almost definitely shoujo
